'''Dean Trevor Busby'''<ref>Rothmans RL Yearbooks</ref><ref>Gillettet RL Yearbook</ref> (born 1 February 1973) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] and at club level for [[Hull FC]] (two spells), [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and the [[Warrington Wolves]], as a {{rlp|pr|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite book| first1= Graham| last1= Williams| first2= Peter| last2= Lush| first3= David| last3= Farrar| title= The British Rugby League Records Book| publisher= London League| isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6| year= 2009| pages= 108–114}}</ref>

==Background==
Dean Busby was born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Hull FC===
Busby played {{rlp|lf}} in [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]'s 14–4 victory over [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[1990–91 Rugby League Premiership|1990–91 Premiership]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Sunday 12 May 1991.<ref name="Caught in time - Hull win 1991 RL Premiership">{{cite web|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/more_sport/article1434554.ece|title=Caught in time - Hull win 1991 RL Premiership|publisher=[[The Times]]|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

===St Helens===
Busby played {{rlp|lf}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Dean Busby played during St. Helens' victory in 1996's [[Super League I]].

===Warrington===
Busby made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves]] on Sunday, 14 February 1999, and he played his last match for the Warrington Wolves on Sunday, 8 September 2002.

===International honours===
Busby won three caps for Great Britain under-21s, one cap for England against Wales in 1992. He won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at Warrington in 2000 against South Africa, in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Cook Islands, Lebanon, and Papua New Guinea, and 2001 against England.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Busby played for Wales in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]], and the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].
